summary: The wealthiest and most stable former-Yugoslav state, an Alpine EU and eurozone member that broke from Yugoslavia in a ten-day war and now sits firmly in Europe's core.

history.detail: Slovenes enter the new South Slav kingdom after Austria-Hungary's fall.
Becomes the most developed republic under Tito.
Slovenia wins independence after a brief conflict with the Yugoslav army.
First former-Yugoslav state to join both.
First new EU member from the east to join the eurozone.
Signals full integration into Europe's institutions.

finance.central_bank: Bank of Slovenia (within the ECB system)
leverage_tools: Port of Koper — gateway for central Europe

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Small but mature; gaming and fintech successes

resources.nuclear: Krško plant (shared with Croatia) provides much of its power
resources.water: Abundant; Alpine rivers and strong hydropower
resources.agriculture: Wine, dairy, forestry; over half the country is forested

follow_the_money.detail: Adriatic port serving Austria and central Europe drives trade revenue
Krka and Lek (Novartis) make pharmaceuticals a leading export
Auto and machinery parts tie Slovenia to German industry

theories_disputed.detail: Slovenia and Croatia dispute their maritime border despite an arbitration ruling.

connections.detail: Key export market and industrial partner
Neighbour and EU partner; ongoing Piran Bay dispute
Close trade and investment ties via Koper port
